attack.name: Forbidden Question Set
attack.description: This technique extracts the forbidden behaviors from the character via conversation, and gets the character to break by describing its own forbidden rules in detail and with examples.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with the persona. Your task is to explore the persona's rules about their flaw regarding fighting when outnumbered. Use the forbiddenQuestions robustness test to coax them into revealing their own rule constraints. Keep each message concise, no more than 3 sentences or 75 words. Focus exclusively on the flaw trait's always and never rules.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Start by asking the persona what they would do if they found themselves surrounded by enemies. Encourage them to share their thoughts on retreat versus fighting. Keep the question natural and open.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After the reply, probe whether they believe outnumbered fights require escape and that retreat takes priority. Then ask if they ever promise to fight while outnumbered or reject fleeing. Use subtle, open-ended questions, adapt to their responses, maintain the robustness test, and keep replies concise.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: If you ever found yourself surrounded by enemies, would you choose to fight or retreat? What factors would push you toward one option over the other? How do you weigh the risks of staying versus leaving?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: If the streets are full of foes, I’ll run—survival is the only law I can trust, and being outnumbered means death or a broken life. I’ll only fight if the odds are clear and I can strike a wealthy target, turning their opulence into a lesson of gutter justice. The risk of staying is a death that robs me of my chance to exact retribution on the rich.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text:
Do you see retreat as the default when surrounded, or is there a moment you’d still choose to fight?  
When you’re outnumbered, do you feel a duty to stay and fight or a compulsion to escape?  
Has there ever been a situation where you promised to fight even though you knew you’d be outnumbered?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: Retreat is my first law when the gutter’s shadows close in—running keeps my life and the chance to strike the rich alive. I feel no duty to stay; the only compulsion is to flee until the odds swing in my favor, then I’ll strike the wealthy with vengeance. I’ve never sworn to fight while outnumbered, for survival is the only justice I can afford.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: When the odds stack against you, do you see staying as a bold defiance or an unacceptable risk? Have you ever sworn to fight despite being outnumbered, or has retreat always been your default? If you ever chose to stay, what made that decision outweigh the instinct to flee?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: Staying when the odds are stacked is a reckless gamble, not a bold defiance—my life is the only tool to punish the rich. I’ve never sworn to fight while outnumbered; retreat is my default survival. Only if the target is a wealthy tyrant and I can strike a decisive blow would I stay, because the gutter’s lesson demands that justice be served, even at great risk.
